hero.title: Building Bridges Beyond Boundaries
hero.subtitle: AUF's partnerships with institutions, industries, and international networks drive innovation, enrich academic programs, and open doors for students and faculty worldwide.

guidelines.title: Guidelines on Establishing Partnership with AUF
guidelines.description: AUF welcomes collaborative relationships with local and international institutions, industries, government agencies, and non-governmental organizations. All partnerships must align with AUF's vision, mission, and institutional priorities, and must undergo the standard partnership establishment process outlined below.

policy.title: AUF Policy for External Affairs
policy.summary: AUF's policy for external affairs governs all institutional partnerships and collaborative agreements. It ensures that partnerships serve the university's academic mission, uphold ethical standards, and are mutually beneficial to both parties.
principles: Partnerships must contribute to the quality of academic programs, research, or community service
All partnership agreements must undergo legal review and be signed by an authorized university representative
Partnerships are established for a defined period and are subject to annual monitoring and evaluation
Both parties must fulfill their respective obligations as stipulated in the signed agreement
AUF reserves the right to terminate any partnership that violates its policies, values, or applicable laws
Confidentiality of proprietary information shared between parties must be upheld at all times

flow.title: Expression of Interest
flow.details: The prospective partner submits a letter of intent or inquiry to the AUF External Affairs Office, indicating the nature, scope, and objectives of the proposed collaboration.

flow.title: Submission of Partnership Proposal
flow.details: AUF provides the official Partnership Proposal Form. The prospective partner completes and submits the form along with supporting documents including their organization profile and proposed terms of collaboration.

flow.title: Evaluation and Assessment
flow.personInCharge: VP for External Affairs · Relevant College Dean
flow.details: The External Affairs Committee, in consultation with the relevant dean or department head, evaluates the proposal for institutional alignment, feasibility, and mutual benefit.

flow.title: Endorsement and Approval
flow.details: Approved proposals are endorsed by the VP for External Affairs to the University President for final approval. Major or multi-year agreements may require Board of Trustees ratification.

flow.title: MOA / MOU Drafting and Legal Review
flow.details: The University Counsel drafts or reviews the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), ensuring all terms, obligations, and conditions comply with applicable laws and AUF policies.

flow.title: Signing Ceremony
flow.details: The finalized agreement is formally signed by authorized representatives of both parties. The External Affairs Office coordinates the ceremony and retains official copies of the signed document.

flow.title: Implementation and Coordination
flow.details: The relevant college or administrative office executes the partnership activities per the signed agreement. The External Affairs Office maintains oversight and facilitates inter-institutional communication.

flow.title: Monitoring, Evaluation, and Renewal
flow.details: Annual monitoring and evaluation are conducted for all active partnerships. Partners are notified three months before expiration for renewal discussions. Non-performing partnerships may be terminated by either party with proper written notice.

guidelines.contactNote: For partnership inquiries, contact the External Affairs Office at externalaffairs@auf.edu.ph or (045) 888-8691 local 108.

forms.title: Partnership Forms
forms.description: Download the official AUF partnership and engagement forms below. All completed forms must be submitted to the External Affairs Office for processing. Ensure all required fields are properly accomplished before submission.
forms.submissionNote: Submit completed forms to the External Affairs Office, Main Building Ground Floor, or email them to externalaffairs@auf.edu.ph.

networks.title: Networks & Associations
networks.description: AUF is an active member of prestigious international and national academic networks that promote quality assurance, academic excellence, institutional transparency, and cross-border collaboration among universities.

memberships.name: ASEAN University Network Quality Assurance
memberships.description: AUN-QA is the regional quality assurance framework for universities within the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN). Membership involves rigorous quality assessments of academic programs and institutional-level reviews conducted by international peer evaluators.
memberships.whatItMeans: AUF's participation in AUN-QA demonstrates its commitment to meeting internationally recognized standards for curriculum design, teaching quality, student outcomes, and institutional governance.

memberships.name: Association of Universities of Asia and the Pacific
memberships.description: AUAP is a multilateral consortium of universities across the Asia-Pacific region committed to promoting educational cooperation, research exchange, and academic mobility. Member universities collaborate on joint programs, conferences, and student exchange initiatives.
memberships.whatItMeans: Through AUAP, AUF students and faculty gain access to academic exchange programs, international conferences, and collaborative research opportunities with partner universities across Asia and the Pacific.

memberships.name: Philippine Accrediting Association of Schools, Colleges, and Universities
memberships.description: PAASCU is the foremost voluntary accreditation agency for private schools, colleges, and universities in the Philippines. AUF's accredited programs undergo periodic quality assessments covering faculty qualifications, curriculum, facilities, research output, and student services.
memberships.whatItMeans: PAASCU accreditation is a mark of academic excellence recognized by CHED and by employers nationwide. AUF programs with PAASCU Level III or IV accreditation enjoy deregulated status and additional government benefits.

memberships.name: Philippine Association of Colleges and Universities Commission on Accreditation
memberships.description: PACUCOA provides voluntary accreditation for private higher education institutions in the Philippines. AUF participates in PACUCOA's accreditation process for select academic programs, ensuring they meet defined quality benchmarks.
memberships.whatItMeans: PACUCOA accreditation signals to students, parents, and employers that AUF's programs meet rigorous, independently validated standards of quality.
